mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously whenever rate trajectories are observable.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a 3-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence. This interaction effect predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days because both demand components must deteriorate simultaneously to trigger the mechanism.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 735904M as of 2025-11-01, HOUST at 1246K as of 2025-10-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances (both RSAFS and HOUST declining), or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines by more than 1% over a 2-month window, signaling supply-side weakness and reduced corporate capacity utilization. This triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings revisions reflect lower output capacity.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 101.7935 (revised to 101.0344) as of 2025-11-01
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following INDPRO declines are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 50,000 persons per month, signaling labor market weakness that precedes broader economic slowdown. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 159526K (revised to 158432K) as of 2025-12-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite weak payroll growth, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 3% annualized in a quarter, signaling robust demand expansion. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as corporate earnings benefit from strong economic activity and capacity utilization increases.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 24024.957B (revised to 24026.834B) as of 2025-07-01
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in high-growth quarters are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 3% year-over-year, the joint restrictive monetary stance signals sustained policy tightening. This interaction effect predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days because both conditions must hold simultaneously to trigger the mechanism.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.72% as of 2025-12-01, CPIAUCSL at 326.030 as of 2025-12-01
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances (both FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and inflation elevated), or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1,000 thousand units annualized, signaling residential construction weakness that reduces near-term demand for materials, labor, and financing. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as forward construction activity expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1246K (revised to 1273K) as of 2025-10-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite weak housing starts, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
